description: Add a batch config.setToolsEnabled RPC so group-level toggles perform one config write instead of N individual writes. Follows the exact same pattern as the existing setAllToolsEnabled path.

userStories.title: Add ConfigSetToolsEnabledParams to shared types
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want a shared param type for the batch tool toggle RPC so that the server and extension use the same interface.
acceptanceCriteria: ConfigSetToolsEnabledParams interface exists in platform/shared/src/index.ts with fields: plugin (string), tools (string[]), enabled (boolean)
JSDoc comment describes it as config.setToolsEnabled request params

userStories.title: Add handleConfigSetToolsEnabled to the MCP server
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want a server-side handler for config.setToolsEnabled that mutates N tools in one pass with a single config persist and broadcast.
acceptanceCriteria: It validates params: plugin (string), tools (string[]), enabled (boolean)
It verifies the plugin exists in the registry
It verifies every tool name in the array exists in the plugin (returns error if any is missing)
It mutates state.toolConfig for all specified tools in a single loop
It calls onToolConfigChanged and onToolConfigPersist exactly once
It sends one plugins.changed broadcast and one { ok: true } response
The config.setToolsEnabled method is routed in extension-protocol.ts

userStories.title: Add batch tool toggle to extension background and bridge
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the extension background and side panel bridge to support a batch setToolsEnabled message so the side panel can toggle a group of tools with one RPC.
acceptanceCriteria: BgSetToolsEnabledMessage interface exists in extension-messages.ts with type 'bg:setToolsEnabled', plugin (string), tools (string[]), enabled (boolean)
BgSetToolsEnabledMessage is included in the InternalMessage union type
handleBgSetToolsEnabled handler exists in background-message-handlers.ts following the same pattern as handleBgSetAllToolsEnabled
The handler optimistically updates all specified tools in the server state cache
The handler uses addPendingPluginAllToolsUpdate/removePendingPluginAllToolsUpdate for concurrency protection
The handler sends a single config.setToolsEnabled RPC (not N individual RPCs)
On failure, the handler surgically reverts only the specified tools using captured original states
The handler is registered in the messageHandlers map with key 'bg:setToolsEnabled'
setToolsEnabled function exists in bridge.ts sending { type: 'bg:setToolsEnabled', plugin, tools, enabled }
userStories.notes:
Four files to modify:

1. platform/browser-extension/src/extension-messages.ts — Add after BgSetAllToolsEnabledMessage (line 133):

```ts
/** Side panel → Background: toggle a subset of tools for a plugin */
export interface BgSetToolsEnabledMessage {
  type: 'bg:setToolsEnabled';
  plugin: string;
  tools: string[];
  enabled: boolean;
}
```

Add BgSetToolsEnabledMessage to the InternalMessage union (line 179-204).

2. platform/browser-extension/src/background-message-handlers.ts — Add handleBgSetToolsEnabled after handleBgSetAllToolsEnabled (line 421). Follow the same pattern as handleBgSetAllToolsEnabled (lines 372-421) but scope to the specified tool names:

- Read `message.tools as string[]` for the tool name list
- Capture original enabled states for only those tools (for surgical rollback)
- Optimistically update only those tools in the cache
- Use addPendingPluginAllToolsUpdate(plugin, toolNames, enabled) — this function already accepts an array of tool names, it's not all-tools-specific despite its name
- Send one `sendServerRequest('config.setToolsEnabled', { plugin, tools: toolNames, enabled })`
- On success: removePendingPluginAllToolsUpdate and sendResponse
- On failure: removePendingPluginAllToolsUpdate, revert only the specified tools, sendResponse error

Register in the messageHandlers map (around line 556-559): `['bg:setToolsEnabled', handleBgSetToolsEnabled]`

Add to the export block at the bottom.

3. platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/bridge.ts — Add after setAllToolsEnabled (line 122):

```ts
const setToolsEnabled = (plugin: string, tools: string[], enabled: boolean): Promise<unknown> =>
  sendBgMessage({ type: 'bg:setToolsEnabled', plugin, tools, enabled });
```

Add setToolsEnabled to the export object (around line 155-180).

userStories.title: Use setToolsEnabled in PluginCard group toggle
userStories.description: As a user, I want toggling a tool group to perform a single batch operation so that config writes are efficient.
acceptanceCriteria: handleToggleGroup in PluginCard.tsx calls setToolsEnabled(plugin.name, toolNames, checked) instead of Promise.all(map(setToolEnabled))
The group toggle sends exactly one RPC call regardless of group size
Optimistic UI update still works: all tools in the group toggle instantly on click
Rollback on failure still works: all tools in the group revert to their original state
